title: Cream Cheese Garlic Bread Korean

seo.title: Best Korean Cream Cheese Garlic Bread Recipe (Easy!)

seo.imageAlt: Freshly baked Korean cream cheese garlic bread with a golden crust and creamy filling, ready to serve.
seo.imageCaption: Indulge in the viral sensation: homemade Korean Cream Cheese Garlic Bread, perfectly golden and oozing with sweet, savory cream cheese.

description: Experience the viral sensation of Korean Cream Cheese Garlic Bread, a delightful fusion of sweet and savory flavors. Soft, pillowy bread is generously filled with a creamy, slightly sweet cheese mixture and then dipped in an aromatic garlic butter bath before being baked to golden perfection. This beloved street food snack is an irresistible treat for any time of day.

ingredients.item: Round bread buns (like brioche or milk bread)

instructions.title: Prepare the Cream Cheese Filling
instructions.text: In a medium bowl, combine the softened cream cheese, granulated sugar, heavy cream or milk, and egg yolk. Whisk until the mixture is smooth and creamy with no lumps. Transfer the filling into a piping bag or a ziplock bag with a corner snipped off.

instructions.title: Prepare the Garlic Butter Dip
instructions.text: In another bowl, whisk together the melted unsalted butter, minced garlic, mayonnaise, and condensed milk or honey. Mix well until all ingredients are thoroughly combined. This creates the rich, savory, and slightly sweet garlic butter that coats the bread.

instructions.title: Prepare the Bread
instructions.text: Preheat your oven to 180°C (350°F). Take each round bread bun and make 6-8 deep cuts, forming a criss-cross pattern. Be careful not to cut all the way through the bottom of the bread, ensuring the base remains intact to hold the filling.

instructions.title: Fill the Bread with Cream Cheese
instructions.text: Carefully open up the cuts in each bread bun. Pipe the cream cheese filling generously into each crevice. Make sure to get the filling deep into the cuts for a satisfying burst of flavor in every bite. If you have any leftover filling, you can pipe it on top.

instructions.title: Dip and Coat with Garlic Butter
instructions.text: Gently pick up each filled bread bun and dip it entirely into the prepared garlic butter mixture, or generously spoon the mixture over the bread, ensuring all sides and crevices are well coated. Place the coated bread buns on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.

instructions.title: Bake to Golden Perfection
instructions.text: Bake the cream cheese garlic bread in the preheated oven for 15-20 minutes, or until the bread is golden brown and the cream cheese filling is bubbly and slightly caramelized. Keep an eye on it to prevent burning. Once baked, remove from the oven.

instructions.title: Garnish and Serve
instructions.text: Sprinkle the baked Korean Cream Cheese Garlic Bread generously with dried parsley flakes for both color and a hint of fresh herb flavor. Serve immediately while warm, as the warm, gooey filling and fragrant garlic butter are at their best.

tips: For the best texture, use soft, slightly sweet bread like brioche or milk bread buns. Stale bread will not absorb the garlic butter as well.
Ensure your cream cheese is at room temperature before mixing to achieve a smooth, lump-free filling. Cold cream cheese will be difficult to work with.
Serve the Korean Cream Cheese Garlic Bread warm for the ultimate experience, as the filling is gooey and the garlic butter is fragrant. Reheat gently in an oven if needed.
article:
Prepare your taste buds for an unforgettable culinary journey! In the world of viral food trends, few dishes have captured hearts and appetites quite like Korean Cream Cheese Garlic Bread. This delectable treat, a staple in Korean bakeries and cafes, offers a delightful twist on traditional garlic bread, transforming it into a sweet, savory, and incredibly rich experience. If you’re a fan of garlic, cheese, and a touch of sweetness, get ready to discover your new obsession. We’ll guide you through everything you need to know about making this sensational bread right in your own kitchen, bringing a piece of Korean baking culture to your home.

## What is Korean Cream Cheese Garlic Bread?

At its core, Korean Cream Cheese Garlic Bread is a symphony of contrasting flavors and textures. Unlike its Italian counterpart, which is typically savory and served as an accompaniment, this Korean version stands proudly as a star dish, often enjoyed as a snack, dessert, or a luxurious breakfast item. It features a soft, often brioche-like bun that is deeply scored, then generously filled with a sweet, tangy cream cheese mixture. The entire bun is then drenched in a fragrant, buttery garlic sauce, often sweetened with condensed milk or honey, before being baked to golden perfection. The result is an irresistible combination of crispy edges, a tender interior, a creamy, sweet-and-sour center, and an aromatic garlic butter coating that makes every bite an explosion of flavor. Its popularity surged thanks to social media, showcasing its beautiful presentation and incredibly satisfying taste, making Korean Cream Cheese Garlic Bread a must-try for any food enthusiast.

## Ingredients You'll Need

Making this viral sensation requires a few key components, but don't worry – most ingredients are readily available.

For the Bread:
*   6-8 round bread buns (brioche buns, dinner rolls, or even French bread slices work well; traditional Korean bakeries use a soft, slightly sweet bun)

For the Cream Cheese Filling:
*   250g cream cheese, softened
*   50g powdered sugar (confectioners' sugar)
*   1 tablespoon milk (or heavy cream for a richer filling)

For the Garlic Butter Coating:
*   100g unsalted butter, melted
*   4-6 cloves garlic, minced very finely
*   2 tablespoons mayonnaise (adds richness and helps emulsify)
*   2 tablespoons condensed milk or honey (for sweetness, condensed milk is traditional)
*   1 large egg, whisked
*   1/2 teaspoon dried parsley flakes (for garnish and flavor)
*   Pinch of salt (to balance the sweetness)

For Garnish (Optional):
*   Fresh parsley, chopped

## Step-by-Step Instructions

Follow these steps to create your own delicious Korean Cream Cheese Garlic Bread:

1.  **Prepare the Buns:** If using whole round buns, use a sharp serrated knife to cut them into 6 or 8 wedges, but do not cut all the way through the base. The goal is to create deep scores that hold the filling and absorb the garlic butter, but keep the bun intact at the bottom. Set aside.

2.  **Make the Cream Cheese Filling:** In a medium bowl, combine the softened cream cheese, powdered sugar, and milk (or heavy cream). Mix until smooth and creamy. Transfer the filling into a piping bag (or a ziploc bag with a corner snipped off) for easier application.

3.  **Fill the Buns:** Carefully open the scored sections of each bun and pipe a generous amount of the cream cheese filling into each crevice. Be sure to get it deep into the cuts. You want a good amount of filling in every bite.

4.  **Prepare the Garlic Butter Coating:** In another bowl, combine the melted butter, finely minced garlic, mayonnaise, condensed milk (or honey), whisked egg, dried parsley, and a pinch of salt. Mix thoroughly until all ingredients are well combined and the mixture is emulsified.

5.  **Drench the Buns:** Dip each filled bun into the garlic butter mixture. Ensure every surface, especially the top and sides, is generously coated. You can also spoon the mixture over the buns if dipping is too messy. Place the coated buns on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.

6.  **Bake:** Preheat your oven to 160°C (325°F). Bake the Korean Cream Cheese Garlic Bread for 15-20 minutes, or until the buns are golden brown and the cream cheese filling is warm and slightly gooey. The garlic butter should be bubbling and fragrant.

7.  **Garnish and Serve:** Once out of the oven, let them cool for a few minutes. Garnish with fresh chopped parsley, if desired. Serve warm and enjoy immediately!

## Pro Tips for the Best Results

Achieving that perfect bakery-quality Korean Cream Cheese Garlic Bread is easier with a few insider tricks:

*   **Bread Choice Matters:** While brioche buns are ideal for their softness and slight sweetness, any soft, slightly dense dinner roll will work. Avoid very airy or crusty bread as it won't absorb the flavors as well.
*   **Don't Skimp on Scoring:** Deep cuts are crucial! They allow the cream cheese filling to nestle in properly and ensure the garlic butter soaks into the bread's interior, not just the surface.
*   **Soften Cream Cheese Properly:** Room temperature cream cheese mixes smoothly, preventing lumps in your filling.
*   **Emulsify the Garlic Butter:** Whisk the garlic butter mixture thoroughly. The mayonnaise helps to emulsify the butter and egg, creating a thicker, richer coating that adheres better to the bread.
*   **Generous Coating:** Don't be shy with the garlic butter! The more, the merrier. This is what gives the bread its signature glossy finish and intense flavor.
*   **Bake at a Lower Temperature:** A slightly lower oven temperature prevents the garlic from burning while allowing the bread to heat through and the cream cheese to melt beautifully.
*   **Serve Warm:** Korean Cream Cheese Garlic Bread is best enjoyed warm, straight from the oven, when the cheese is gooey and the bread is soft and fragrant.

## Serving Suggestions

This versatile bread can be enjoyed in many ways. It’s perfect as a standalone snack or a decadent breakfast item with a cup of coffee or tea. In Korea, it's often found in cafes and bakeries, enjoyed as a treat. You can also serve it as a unique appetizer for a brunch party or even as a surprisingly delightful side dish to a light salad, offering a sweet and savory contrast. Its richness means a single bun can be quite filling, making it a satisfying indulgence on its own.

## Variations & Substitutions

Feel free to get creative and personalize your Korean Cream Cheese Garlic Bread:

*   **Spicy Kick:** Add a pinch of red pepper flakes to the garlic butter for a subtle heat.
*   **Herbal Infusion:** Experiment with other dried herbs like oregano or thyme in the garlic butter.
*   **Different Cheeses:** While cream cheese is traditional, you could try mixing in a small amount of mozzarella or Parmesan for a different texture and flavor profile, though this will alter the classic taste.
*   **Sweetness Levels:** Adjust the amount of powdered sugar in the cream cheese and condensed milk/honey in the garlic butter to suit your preference.
*   **Vegan Option:** Use plant-based cream cheese, vegan butter, and a flax egg substitute (1 tbsp ground flaxseed + 3 tbsp water) for the egg in the garlic butter. Ensure your bread buns are also vegan.
*   **No Mayonnaise:** If you dislike mayonnaise, you can omit it, but the texture of the garlic butter might be slightly thinner, and it will lack some of the richness.
*   **Bread Alternatives:** Sliced baguette or even a larger loaf of artisan bread can be used, simply adjust the scoring and baking time.

## Frequently Asked Questions

**Q: Can I make Korean Cream Cheese Garlic Bread ahead of time?**
A: You can prepare the cream cheese filling and garlic butter mixture a day in advance and store them in the refrigerator. Assemble and bake just before serving for the best results. Baked bread is best eaten fresh.

**Q: What kind of bread is best for this recipe?**
A: Soft, slightly sweet buns like brioche, milk buns, or a good quality dinner roll work perfectly. The key is a bread that is soft enough to absorb the butter and hold its shape after scoring.

**Q: Is Korean Cream Cheese Garlic Bread spicy?**
A: No, the traditional version is not spicy. It's a balance of sweet, savory, and tangy flavors. However, you can add red pepper flakes to the garlic butter if you prefer a spicy kick.

**Q: How do I store leftovers?**
A: Store any leftover bread in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 1-2 days, or in the refrigerator for up to 3-4 days. Reheat gently in a microwave or oven for a few minutes until warm.

**Q: Can I use fresh garlic instead of minced garlic from a jar?**
A: Absolutely! Freshly minced garlic will always provide the best flavor. Just make sure it’s very finely minced to blend well into the butter mixture.

Embrace the joy of Korean baking and treat yourself to this incredible Korean Cream Cheese Garlic Bread. Its unique blend of sweet, savory, and creamy textures is sure to become a cherished recipe in your home. Happy baking!
